skill.name: unit-testing-test-generate
skill.description: Generate comprehensive, maintainable unit tests across languages with strong coverage and edge case focus.
skill.summary: Automatically generate high-quality unit tests for Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, and Go with edge case coverage and proper mocking.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 31 patterns but all are false positives. The skill is documentation-only containing code examples in markdown format. The subprocess.run pattern is legitimate test coverage functionality. Cryptographic warnings are Jest describe() functions. No executable code poses security risks.

content.value_statement: Writing unit tests manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates test generation across multiple languages and frameworks with edge case coverage and proper mocking.
actual_capabilities: Generate pytest tests for Python code with fixtures and mocks
Create Jest test suites for JavaScript and TypeScript projects
Analyze code structure to identify testable functions and classes
Generate React component tests with Testing Library patterns
Detect coverage gaps and generate targeted tests for uncovered code
Create mock objects and fixtures for external dependencies
limitations: Focused on unit testing only, not integration or E2E tests
Requires accessible source code for analysis
Generated tests may need customization for domain-specific validation
Does not replace manual review of edge cases unique to business logic

use_cases.title: Rapid test suite creation for new projects
use_cases.description: Quickly bootstrap comprehensive test coverage when starting a new codebase or adding tests to legacy code without existing tests.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building new APIs or services

use_cases.title: Maintain test coverage during refactoring
use_cases.description: Regenerate tests after significant code changes to ensure coverage remains high and edge cases are still validated.

use_cases.title: Learn testing best practices by example
use_cases.description: Study generated tests to understand proper mocking strategies, assertion patterns, and test organization for your framework.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Python test generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate pytest unit tests for the Python module at ./src/utils.py. Include tests for all public functions with happy path and error cases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick test generation for a utility module

prompt_templates.title: JavaScript Jest test suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Jest tests for the functions in ./helpers/validation.js. Use proper mocking for external API calls and include edge cases for null and undefined inputs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing validation logic with external dependencies

prompt_templates.title: React component testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate React component tests for ./components/UserProfile.tsx using Testing Library. Test rendering, props handling, and user interactions like button clicks.

prompt_templates.title: Coverage gap analysis and test generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze coverage report from ./coverage/coverage.json and generate targeted tests for all uncovered lines in the src/models/ directory. Focus on boundary conditions and exception handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving coverage for existing test suites

output_examples.input: Generate pytest tests for a calculator module with add, subtract, multiply functions
output_examples.output: Test file with TestCalculator class containing parametrized tests for each operation, edge cases for zero and negative numbers, and type error handling

output_examples.input: Create Jest tests for an async API client with mock fetch
output_examples.output: Test suite with mocked fetch responses, tests for success and error states, proper async/await patterns, and timeout handling

best_practices: Always review generated tests to ensure assertions validate correct expected behavior
Add domain-specific edge cases that automated analysis may not detect
Run generated tests immediately to verify they pass before committing
anti_patterns: Do not commit generated tests without reviewing and understanding each assertion
Avoid generating tests for code that is rapidly changing - wait for stability
Do not rely solely on generated tests for security-critical or financial calculations

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: Primary support for Python (pytest), JavaScript and TypeScript (Jest). Also covers Java (JUnit) and Go (testing package) with framework-specific patterns.

faq.question: Can this skill test private methods?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on public API testing. Private methods should be tested indirectly through public method tests. Direct private method testing is generally not recommended.

faq.question: How do I handle database dependencies in generated tests?
faq.answer: The skill generates mock fixtures for database connections. For integration tests requiring real databases, you will need to add database setup and teardown manually.

faq.question: Will the generated tests work with my existing CI pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, generated tests use standard framework conventions compatible with common CI tools like GitHub Actions, Jenkins, and GitLab CI.

faq.question: How can I verify the quality of generated tests?
faq.answer: Run your coverage tool after executing generated tests. Aim for high statement and branch coverage. Review that assertions check meaningful conditions.

faq.question: Can this skill update existing test files?
faq.answer: The skill generates new test code. You can manually merge generated tests with existing files or create separate test modules for new functionality.
